audit_items.proof_trace: Proof fails: validate_kzg_g1 (bytes.c:90) explicitly accepts point-at-infinity commitments without rejection. No additional infinity check exists before line 910 aggregation. Point-at-infinity contributes zero to weighted sum but corresponding proof remains in equation, creating exploitable imbalance in pairing verification.
audit_items.attack_scenario: Attacker sends P2P data availability sample with commitment=0xc00000...(infinity) plus arbitrary cell data and proof. Batch verification accepts the infinity commitment (bytes.c:90), aggregates it into random linear combination where it contributes zero, while weighted proof remains. Invalid cell data passes verification because commitment term vanishes from pairing equation.
